violations.description: Latency budget check uses Date.now() wall-clock elapsed time, which can vary under CI host load and create non-deterministic failures near threshold.
violations.suggestion: Keep the latency assertion but add bounded slack or capture service timing metrics for a more stable threshold gate.
violations.code_snippet: const start = Date.now(); ... const elapsedMs = Date.now() - start; expect(elapsedMs).toBeLessThanOrEqual(storyE5Context.latencyBudgetMs);

recommendations: Use a stable latency assertion strategy for CI-host variance (bounded slack or service-timing metric assertions)
Retain deterministic provider event IDs and signed header generation approach already present
Keep avoiding hard waits and conditional control flow in test bodies
summary: Determinism is strong overall; one medium-risk wall-clock timing assertion may become flaky under noisy CI nodes.
